summary.text: Temporary type fixing to allow any level of orders until we get to properly clean all the types
An object that is used to configure how an entity is retrieved from the database. It accepts as a typed parameter an
`Entity`

class,
which provides correct typing of field names in its properties.
An array of strings, each being attribute names of the entity to retrieve in the result.
A number indicating the number of records to skip before retrieving the results.
A number indicating the number of records to return in the result.

An array of strings, each being relation names of the entity to retrieve in the result.

You can only retrieve data models defined in the same module. To retrieve linked data models
from other modules, use [Query](https://docs.medusajs.com/learn/fundamentals/module-links/query) instead.
An object used to specify how to sort the returned records. Its keys are the names of attributes of the entity, and a key's value can either be
`ASC`
to sort retrieved records in an ascending order, or
`DESC`
to sort retrieved records in a descending order.
A boolean indicating whether deleted records should also be retrieved as part of the result. This only works if the entity extends the
`SoftDeletableEntity`
class.
Enable ORM specific defined filters
Enable ORM specific defined options

An object used to allow specifying flexible queries with and/or conditions.
An array of filters to apply on the entity, where each item in the array is joined with an "and" condition.
An array of filters to apply on the entity, where each item in the array is joined with an "or" condition.

The ID of the event's group. Grouped events are useful when you have distributed transactions
where you need to explicitly group, release and clear events upon lifecycle events of a transaction.

When set, you must release the grouped events using the Event Module's
`releaseGroupedEvents`
method to emit the events.
The event's name.
Additional meadata to pass with the event.
The data payload that subscribers receive. For example, the ID or IDs of the created user. (e.g. { id: "123" } or { ids: ["123", "456"] })
The details of an event to emit.
In an inverted relationship the foreign key is on the other service and the primary key is on the current service
Force the relationship to return a list

If the module is a link module

If true it expands a RemoteQuery property but doesn't create a pivot table

Fields that will be part of the link record aside from the primary keys that can be updated
If not explicitly defined, this array will be populated by databaseConfig.extraFields
Name of the pivot table. If not provided it is auto generated
Prefix for the id column. If not provided it is "link"
If true, the link joiner will cascade deleting the relationship

A context used to share resources, such as transaction manager, between the application and the module.
An instance of a transaction manager of type
`TManager`
, which is a typed parameter passed to the context to specify the type of the
`transactionManager`
An instance of a manager, typically an entity manager, of type
`TManager`
, which is a typed parameter passed to the context to specify the type of the
`manager`
A string indicating the isolation level of the context. Possible values are
`READ UNCOMMITTED`
`READ COMMITTED`
`REPEATABLE READ`
, or
`SERIALIZABLE`
A boolean value indicating whether nested transactions are enabled.
A string indicating the ID of the group to aggregate the events to be emitted at a later point.
A string indicating the ID of the current transaction.
A string indicating the ID of the current run.
An instance of a message aggregator, which is used to aggregate messages to be emitted at a later point.
A string indicating the ID of the current request.
A string indicating the idempotencyKey of the current workflow execution.
A string indicating the idempotencyKey of the parent workflow execution.
preventReleaseEvents
A boolean value indicating whether the current workflow execution is being cancelled.

Weither or not a sub workflow cancellation is being triggered from a parent step.
If true, the parent step will not be triggered by the sub workflow.
The status of a gift card.
Represents a gift card in the Loyalty module.
The gift card's ID.
The unique redemption code for the gift card.
The current status of the gift card.
The monetary value of the gift card.
The three-letter ISO currency code of the gift card's value.
The ID of the customer who owns this gift card.
The customer who owns this gift card.
The ID of the resource this gift card was created from (e.g. an order ID).
An optional note about the gift card.
The resource type this gift card references (e.g. "order").
The date the gift card expires, or null if it does not expire.
The date the gift card was created.
The date the gift card was last updated.
The data required to create a gift card.
The unique redemption code for the gift card. If not provided, one is auto-generated.
The monetary value of the gift card.
The three-letter ISO currency code for the gift card's value.
The expiration date of the gift card, or null if it does not expire.
The ID of the resource this gift card is associated with.
The resource type this gift card is associated with (e.g. "order").
The ID of the line item this gift card was purchased as.
The ID of the customer to associate with the gift card.
Custom key-value pairs to attach to the gift card.
The data required to update a gift card.
The ID of the gift card to update.
The updated monetary value of the gift card.
The updated status of the gift card.
An updated note about the gift card.
The updated three-letter ISO currency code.
The updated expiration date, or null to remove the expiration.
The updated ID of the customer associated with the gift card.
Updated custom key-value pairs attached to the gift card.

The field to order results by.
The main service interface for the Loyalty Module.
This method creates a gift card.

content.text:
```ts
const giftCard = await loyaltyModuleService.createGiftCards({
  code: "UNIQUECODE123",
  value: 100,
  currency_code: "usd",
  line_item_id: "li_123",
  customer_id: "cust_123",
  expires_at: null,
  reference_id: "order_123",
  reference: "order",
  metadata: {},
})
```

summary.text: The gift card to create.
A context used to share resources, such as transaction manager, between the application and the module.
This method creates gift cards.

content.text:
```ts
const giftCards = await loyaltyModuleService.createGiftCards([
  {
    code: "UNIQUECODE123",
    value: 100,
    currency_code: "usd",
    line_item_id: "li_123",
    customer_id: "cust_123",
    expires_at: null,
    reference_id: "order_123",
    reference: "order",
    metadata: {},
  },
])
```
